## [00:00 - 01:07]

I tried so many different career paths, bro. Trying the different career paths is me planting a ton of different seeds, and I got to try a lot of different fruit. And then when I tried the fruit from the seeds that I nurtured, when it tasted bad, I gave myself permission to uproot that tree and throw it away. Imagine you invested your 20s to your 40s and you had one seed, and then you planted the seed and then fruit bears at age 30, and you eat that fruit and you spent 10 years planting that plant and it tastes like shit. And then because of the sunk cost fallacy, you go, I've already spent 10 years planting this tree. There's no point planting other trees, so you spend the next 20 years of your life eating shit fruit. I think that version can still work. Do you think so? If you've planted one tree? I think there's a very small percentage of people out there that just know from the day that they're five or six. Yeah, that's luck, man. This is my seed, and I'm just going to nurture this one seed for the rest of my life. But how rare is that? Come on, man. That is rare. That's like winning the lottery. That's rare. I think the way to go about it when you're young, take a ton of seeds, throw it in the garden, water all of it, throw manure everywhere, and see what grows.
